stem: A right rectangular prism has a height of 12 centimeters. The length of the prism's base is x centimeters, which is 4 centimeters more than the width of the prism's base. Which function V gives the volume of the prism, in cubic centimeters, in terms of the length of the prism's base?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. Since the length is 4 cm more than the width, the width is (x − 4) cm. The base area is x(x − 4) square centimeters, and the volume is base area times height: V(x) = 12 · x(x − 4) = 12x(x − 4).

Choice A and Choice D mistakenly treat 12 as a third dimension rather than a height multiplier. Choice C uses width (x + 4) instead of (x − 4).
